column1: # Shared parity table for the BRIDGE_ADMIN_AGENT_ID roster-value parser. BOTH
# and each must produce the EXPECTED outcome for every row. The expected column
# is the SECURE-POLICY result, which by design equals what bash sourcing would
# produce for every LEGITIMATE shape (verified: every FOUND:<value> row resolves
# to byte-exactly the value `env -i bash` assigns — so we never accept a WRONG
# value, no fail-open), and deliberately fail-closes (MALFORMED) the corrupt /
# concatenation / command-substitution shapes that bash would otherwise honor
# pins shell<->Python agreement on that policy and prevents a future divergence
# where one parser silently accepts a value the other (or bash) reads differently.
# Format: 3 TAB-separated columns. Lines starting with '#' and blank lines are
# comments. The roster-line and expected fields use Python/bash escapes decoded
# by the drivers: \t \v \f \r \n  \\ for a literal backslash. The roster file
# written for each row is exactly: "#!/usr/bin/env bash\n" + <decoded line> + "\n".
# expected is one of: FOUND:<value>  (value may be empty -> "FOUND:")
# r5-round6 review: VT/FF/CR in the LINE indent or after `export` is NOT a bash
# command-blank, so the line is not an assignment to bash/Python — the shell
# matcher must use [[:blank:]] (space+tab) not [[:space:]] so it agrees (NONE).
# legit space/tab indent + export separator still MATCH (regression guard)
